steps.title: Link isotope to temperature
steps.content: F13 had a higher oxygen-18 concentration than M03, and the researchers concluded that F13 formed at a lower temperature. A supporting finding must connect higher oxygen-18 with colder water.

steps.title: Reject same-direction patterns
steps.content: Choice A links lower oxygen-18 with lower temperature. Choice B says oxygen-18 and temperature both decrease as depth increases. Choice D links higher temperature with more oxygen-18. Each pattern works against the conclusion.

steps.title: Choose the inverse pattern
steps.content: If oxygen-18 increases as water temperature decreases with depth, then higher oxygen-18 in F13 supports formation at a lower temperature. The answer is C.
